questions.question: In the context of digital IC design, what is a 'netlist'?
options: A file describing the physical placement of components on a chip.
A textual representation of the logical connectivity between components (e.g., gates, flip-flops, etc.) and their pins.
A document detailing the power consumption requirements of an integrated circuit.
A schematic diagram showing only the analog portions of a mixed-signal IC.
A list of network protocols supported by an embedded processor.

questions.question: In computer architecture, what is the defining characteristic of a 'fully associative cache'?
options: Each memory block has a unique, fixed location in the cache.
Any memory block from main memory can be placed in any cache line.
Cache lines are grouped into sets, and a memory block can be placed in any line within its assigned set.
Data is written simultaneously to both the cache and main memory on every write operation.
It only stores the most recently accessed data blocks.

questions.question: In the physical design and verification of high-performance integrated circuits, what phenomenon is 'crosstalk' primarily concerned with?
options: The unintentional leakage of current between power and ground rails.
The increase in resistance of metal interconnects at higher temperatures.
The coupling of electrical signals between adjacent interconnect wires, leading to noise and potential functional errors.
The degradation of transistor performance due to manufacturing process variations.
The effect of cosmic rays on stored data bits, causing single-event upsets.

questions.question: In modern out-of-order execution processors, what is the primary function of a 'Reorder Buffer' (ROB)?
options: To store predicted branch targets and improve control flow prediction accuracy.
To track the status of in-flight instructions and commit them to architectural state in their original program order.
To temporarily hold the contents of general-purpose registers during context switches.
To prefetch data from main memory into the cache before it is explicitly requested by an instruction.
To manage the virtual-to-physical address translation process for memory accesses.

questions.question: In digital synchronous circuits, what is the primary function of a 'register'?
options: To combine multiple input signals into a single output signal based on a selector.
To amplify weak digital signals to drive larger loads.
To store a group of related bits (often called a 'word' or a 'vector') for one or more clock cycles.
To perform arithmetic operations such as addition and subtraction.
To convert parallel data into serial data.
